title: Bruegel is the European think tank that specialises in economics
gist: Boilerplate self-description of Bruegel, a Brussels economics think tank. No market theses or data; only structural facts about its governance and membership model.

insights.claim: Bruegel is funded by a membership that includes EU Member State governments, international corporations and institutions, a mixed public-private funding base worth flagging when weighing the independence of its economic-policy output.
insights.speaker: Bruegel
insights.so_what: Analysts consuming Bruegel research should note its member-funded model spans governments and corporates when judging potential bias.
insights.quote: Bruegel’s membership includes EU Member State governments, international corporations and institutions.

insights.claim: Bruegel organizes its research around fixed three-year medium-term cycles plus an annual programme, both adopted by its board each September after member consultation, giving a predictable cadence for when its policy agenda is reset.
insights.speaker: Bruegel
insights.so_what: Track Bruegel's September programme adoptions to anticipate which EU economic-policy themes it will push over the coming year.
